description: PlutusV2 cost-model entries for the integerToByteString / byteStringToInteger families (Plutus's `batch4b`) that were prepared in Plutus's source but never carried onto mainnet. Why they're not on mainnet: (1) Plutus PR #6056 (May 2024, https://github.com/IntersectMBO/plutus/pull/6056) added batch4b to V2 at `plominPV` in `builtinsIntroducedIn` explicitly as a test of the 'add builtins to an existing ledger language' upgrade mechanism, NOT as a mainnet deployment plan. (2) `builtinsIntroducedIn` only controls which builtins the language can evaluate under a given PV; the on-chain cost-model values are a separate governance object updated by cost-model update proposals. (3) The Plomin hard fork (PV 10, mainnet epoch 537) was activated by an update proposal that carried only V3's `batch5` cost-model values; no proposal extending V2's cost model with batch4b was ever submitted/passed, so db-sync shows V2 stays at 175 params throughout (cost_model_id 260, active from epoch 526). (4) These costs are not stranded forever: Plutus PR #7223 (July 2025, https://github.com/IntersectMBO/plutus/pull/7223) makes all V1/V2 builtins cumulatively available at `vanRossemPV` (PV 11), so a future comprehensive V1/V2 cost-model update could naturally bundle batch4b in. This overlay is an opt-in sandbox for benchmarks that want to model the hypothetical V2 extension; cost values are the speculative ones previously carried inline at epoch 492 of `epoch-timeline.json`.
